articles.title: Defying Trump, Israel strikes Iranian military and fuel targets after Iranian missile barrage
articles.summary: Israel launched missile strikes on Iran in the early hours of Monday, with dozens of warplanes hitting air-defence sites and military targets in Tehran, Isfahan, Tabriz and Karaj, plus a large petrochemical complex near Mahshahr that the IDF said produces materials for Iran's ballistic-missile program. The operation came in response to Iranian missile attacks on Israel hours earlier and defied public calls from US President Trump not to retaliate. Iranian media reported explosions in several cities and the closure of the country's airports.

articles.title: Israel and Iran exchange missile fire in most serious confrontation since April truce
articles.summary: Iran and Israel traded direct missile fire for the first time since a ceasefire took effect in April, threatening the fragile two-month-old truce. The Israeli military said it intercepted ballistic missiles launched from Iran, with sirens sounding across central and southern Israel. Iran's Revolutionary Guard said the operation was a 'warning' triggered by Israel's strike on Beirut's southern suburbs and that responses would be 'broader' if attacks continued. Iran later announced it was halting operations 'for now,' warning of a 'more crushing' response if Israel resumes strikes on Lebanon or Iranian territory.

articles.title: Netanyahu says Iran attacks halted 'for now,' vows to strike Tehran and Hezbollah if attacked again
articles.summary: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Iranian attacks on Israel had been halted 'for now' but warned that Israel would strike Tehran and Hezbollah targets if fired upon again. An Israeli official said Israel had agreed to stop attacking Iran after Trump's intervention but that the military would continue operating in Lebanon. The IDF said all missiles fired from Iran since Sunday night were intercepted or fell in open areas.

articles.title: Trump insists Iran negotiations continuing despite Israel-Iran strikes
articles.summary: President Trump urged both sides to halt fire, posting that 'Israel and Iran must immediately stop shooting,' and insisted Middle East negotiations were still under way despite the exchange of strikes. Trump told Fox News the missile attacks were 'certainly not going to help negotiations.' Washington is demanding Iran surrender its nuclear material and commit never to obtain a nuclear weapon, while Iran's Revolutionary Guard said the ceasefire had been 'conditional on a cease-fire on all fronts.'

articles.title: Israel kills at least 14 Palestinians in strikes across Gaza
articles.summary: Israeli forces killed at least 14 people in strikes across the Gaza Strip, hitting Khan Younis, Gaza City and Deir el-Balah, as the broader regional escalation overshadowed continued violations of the October 2025 ceasefire. Rights groups and Palestinian officials say strikes have persisted throughout the truce, with the humanitarian situation in the enclave deteriorating.

articles.title: Israeli strike on Beirut's southern suburbs kills two, wounds about 20
articles.summary: An Israeli strike on the southern suburbs of Beirut killed at least two people and wounded around 20, Lebanese state media reported, days after a US-supported Lebanon ceasefire. The strike was the trigger Iran cited for its missile barrage on Israel, with Tehran warning that US bases and Israeli assets across the region would be considered 'legitimate targets.' Israel said it would continue operating in Lebanon even after agreeing to pause strikes on Iran.

articles.title: Houthis fire missiles at Israel from Yemen as regional fronts widen
articles.summary: Yemen's Houthi movement said it launched missiles at Israel, triggering sirens in the Tel Aviv area and adding a third front to the day's exchanges alongside Iran and Hezbollah. The Israeli military reported intercepting a projectile fired from Yemen early Monday. The Iran-aligned group framed the launches as solidarity with Lebanon and the Palestinians amid the renewed escalation.

articles.title: Oil prices spike more than 3% then ease after Iran says operations over
articles.summary: Brent crude rose 3.18% to $96.05 a barrel and US WTI gained 3.46% to $93.67 as Iran and Israel traded strikes, before paring gains—WTI easing back toward $91—after Iran announced it had ended military operations against Israel. The swings underscored the market's sensitivity to any threat to Gulf energy flows and the Strait of Hormuz, even as OPEC+ agreed to raise output targets by 188,000 barrels per day from July.

articles.title: Iran says US is responsible for 'whatever happens in the region'
articles.summary: Iranian foreign ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baghaei said Israel's ally the United States bears responsibility for 'whatever happens in the region,' blaming Washington for the resumption of hostilities through its naval pressure near the Strait of Hormuz and its backing of Israel. Tehran reiterated that American bases and Israeli assets would be treated as legitimate targets if strikes on Lebanon or Iran resumed.

articles.title: Gulf states urge restraint as King Abdullah pushes to shore up US-Iran ceasefire
articles.summary: Arab states urged de-escalation as the Israel-Iran exchange rattled the region, with Jordan's King Abdullah emphasising the need to intensify efforts to strengthen the US-Iran ceasefire agreement. Saudi Arabia's energy minister called for greater 'stabilisation' of global energy markets to avert further losses, reflecting Gulf anxiety that renewed fighting could again disrupt shipping and oil exports through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Analysis: The world watches as Israel turns law into ashes
articles.summary: In a commentary, Middle East Monitor argues the latest Israel-Iran escalation arrives after months of devastating warfare across Gaza and Lebanon, repeated ceasefire violations, and a death toll it says has surpassed 76,000 across multiple theatres. The piece frames international inaction as a collapse of the rules-based order and centres Palestinian voices, including a Gaza survivor describing hunger, cold and the collapse of healthcare under continued Israeli strikes.
